# Interplex — Precision Components for NVL72
Last updated: 2026-04-01

## Company Overview
- **HQ:** Singapore (global manufacturing — Malaysia, China, US, Europe)
- Acquired by Bain Capital in 2018
- Revenue: ~$500M-1B estimated (private, not disclosed)
- CEO: Michael Oubre
- Specialty: precision engineered components — metal stampings, connectors, precision assemblies

## Role in NVL72 Supply Chain
- Interplex supplies **precision metal stampings and structural components** used inside NVL72 compute trays
- Likely components: manifold fittings, brackets, retention hardware, precision-stamped flow path components
- Less likely to supply complete manifold assemblies — more likely individual precision-machined or stamped parts that go INTO the manifold assembly
- Interplex has existing relationships with major server ODMs (Foxconn, Flex, Wistron)

## Key Capabilities
- Metal stamping: tight tolerances, high volume
- Precision machining
- Insert molding (plastic + metal hybrid components)
- Medical-grade manufacturing lines (high cleanliness — relevant to liquid cooling)
- Qualified for automotive and data center thermal applications

## ADC Relevance
- Second-tier supplier intelligence — Interplex parts are inside the manifold, not the manifold itself
- Understanding this tier = understanding where NVL72 tray shortage actually originates
- Not a direct ADC vendor
